mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of observations, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1000K signals a severe contraction in residential investment, presaging a broader economic downturn that depresses equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1000, SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low (below 1%) Fed funds rate and falling unemployment rate (UNRATE dropping over last 3 months) creates a powerful expansionary impulse: cheap credit and strengthening labor market boost corporate profits and risk appetite, driving SPX up over 20 days.
failure_condition: Sign Rank IC of this interaction signal is below 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 2%, the Fed is expected to raise rates; any increase in the fed funds rate from its current level is contractionary and negatively impacts equity valuations over 20 days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL y/y > 2% and FEDFUNDS rises, SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth reflects overall economic output; rising GDP signals sustained expansion, which supports earnings growth and justifies higher equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly Rank IC between GDPC1 change and forward 60-day SPX return is less than 0.1 over 10-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4%, the labor market is extremely tight, leading to wage inflation pressures that force the Fed to tighten, causing a negative impact on equity prices in the short term (5 trading days).
failure_condition: Over rolling 3-year windows, when UNRATE < 4%, the average 5-day SPX return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) points to robust consumer spending and residential investment, two key demand components; this combined expansion lifts corporate revenues and stock returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal's directional accuracy is below 55% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (fed funds rate below 0.5%), housing starts increases are particularly stimulative because cheap mortgages boost demand; this leads to positive SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and HOUST rises, the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10).
